title: Aucellia croceorubens
taxonomy: Aucellia croceorubens is classified as a species of succulent plant within the family Asclepiadaceae. This classification places it within the larger group of Asclepiads, a diverse and widespread family of plants. The specific classification of Aucellia croceorubens is further divided into the genus Aucellia and the species A. croceorubens. This plant is native to the Mediterranean region and is often grown for its unique and attractive flowers. As part of the Asclepiadaceae family, Aucellia croceorubens shares characteristics with other plants that have adapted to thrive in dry and arid environments.
uses: Aucellia croceorubens, also known as the red-spotted pancake or red-spotted club moss, is a plant with potential medicinal uses.
